vote.title: H.Con.Res. 14, As Amended; A concurrent resolution establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2025 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2152
vote.title: Rosen Amdt. No. 2152; To provide tax relief for the middle class.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1989
vote.title: Wyden Amdt. No. 1989; To strike section 2001(b)(4) relating to reconciliation instructions to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives to cut $880,000,000,000 from Medicaid.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2177
vote.title: Warnock Amdt. No. 2177; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to access to health care, which may include legislation preventing reductions in funding for Medicaid that could lead to benefit cuts, coverage loss, or slashed provider payments.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1529
vote.title: Markey Amdt. No. 1529; To preserve access to Social Security's phone service.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1693
vote.title: Baldwin Amdt. No. 1693 ; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preventing a reduction in Medicaid funding that could lead to rural hospital closures, cost increases for individuals with other kinds of insurance, or higher rates of uncompensated care.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 1690
vote.title: Motion to Waive Section 305(b)(2) of the CBA re: Cortez Masto Amdt. No. 1690; To create a point of order against legislation that would increase drug costs for seniors and people with disabilities on Medicare.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2126
vote.title: Sanders Amdt. No. 2126; To make sure the Senate can increase the Federal minimum wage to $17 an hour by a simple majority vote.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1644
vote.title: Kim Amdt. No. 1644; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund to prevent increased barriers to American caregivers, including individuals caring for seniors, children, home care workers, and individuals engaged in the care economy.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2180
vote.title: Hirono Amdt. No. 2180; To prevent DOGE from closing Social Security offices, preserving access to benefits for seniors and people with disabilities.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2107
vote.title: Hickenlooper Amdt. No. 2107; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preventing the use of proceeds from public land sales to reduce the Federal deficit.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2186
vote.title: Ossoff Amdt. No. 2186; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to reversing cuts to the Social Security Administration, which may include cuts ordered by the Department of Government Efficiency or any other cuts to seniors' services.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1760
vote.title: Paul Amdt. No. 1760; To modify the debt limit instruction for the House of Representatives and the Senate.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1646
vote.title: Bennet Amdt. No. 1646; To prevent any disruption in security assistance to Ukraine.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1774
vote.title: Padilla Amdt. No. 1774; Reiterating the importance of the Federal Emergency Management Agency and its continued role in providing nonpartisan and long-term disaster relief to disaster survivors.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1884
vote.title: Schumer Amdt. No. 1884; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preventing Trump's tariffs from increasing the cost of groceries and everyday goods for families.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1466
vote.title: Alsobrooks Amdt. No. 1466; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to prohibiting attacks on Federal employees by protecting legally binding collective bargaining agreements and the right to organize.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1310
vote.title: Warner Amdt. No. 1310; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to strengthening protections for members of the Armed Forces by prohibiting the use of any commercial messaging application to transmit information revealing the timing, sequencing, or weapons to be used during impending United States military operations in foreign countries that may endanger the lives of members of the Armed Forces.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1726
vote.title: Lujan Amdt. No. 1726; To strike the provision relating to instructions to the Committee on Agriculture.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1645
vote.title: Reed Amdt. No. 1645; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preventing reduction in enrollment or benefits for individuals enrolled in Medicaid, including seniors, children, families, individuals with disabilities, veterans, and military families.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1758
vote.title: Merkley Amdt. No. 1758; To create a point of order against legislation that would create more debt over a 30-year period than has accumulated over the past 249 years.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 2035
vote.title: Sullivan Amdt. No. 2035; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting Medicare and Medicaid.

vote.question: On the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Proceed to H.Con.Res. 14; A concurrent resolution establishing the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2025 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.

vote.question: On the Concurrent Resolution
vote.title: S.Con.Res. 7, As Amended; An original concurrent resolution setting forth the congressional budget for the United States Government for fiscal year 2025 and setting forth the appropriate budgetary levels for fiscal years 2026 through 2034.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1207
vote.title: Merkley Amdt. No. 1207; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to ending price gouging on prescription drugs.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 922
vote.title: Lee Amdt. No. 922; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to Congress continuing its work to rein in the administrative state by supporting legislation that prevents Federal agencies from finalizing major rules without congressional approval, strengthens the Article 1 law-making powers of Congress, cuts spending resulting from costly regulations, reduces inflation, and unleashes economic growth.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 957
vote.title: Lujan Amdt. No. 957; To strike reconciliation instructions requiring damaging cuts to programs critical to rural Americans and food assistance for American families.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 659
vote.title: Blumenthal Amdt. No. 659; To ensure full and uninterrupted funding for Department of Veterans Affairs health care and benefits provided by the Sergeant First Class Heath Robinson Honoring our Promise to Address Comprehensive Toxics Act of 2022 (Public Law 117-168), also known as the "PACT Act", preventing any cuts or delays.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 971
vote.title: Duckworth Amdt. No. 971; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting access to fertility services, and eliminating barriers for families in need of high-quality, affordable fertility services by expanding nationwide coverage for in vitro fertilization.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 699
vote.title: Lujan Amdt. No. 699; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to supporting police, which may include initiatives that provide funding directly to law enforcement agencies to hire or rehire additional career law enforcement officers in an effort to increase their community policing capacity and crime prevention efforts.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 436
vote.title: Shaheen Amdt. No. 436; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preserving and extending vital tax credits enacted under the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, which make heath care accessible and affordable and that have led to the lowest uninsured rate in our Nation's history.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 233
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Van Hollen Amdt. No. 233; To create a point of order against legislation that would cut funding from the school lunch or school breakfast programs.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 664
vote.title: Slotkin Amdt. No. 664; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to preventing reductions in funding and staffing necessary to respond to, control, and prevent avian flu.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 999
vote.title: Paul Amdt No. 999; To require an adequate amount of deficit reduction as part of reconciliation.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 299
vote.title: Reed Amdt. No. 299; To ensure continued United States support for the Government of Ukraine to stand firm against aggression by the Government of Russia in Europe.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 172
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Reed Amdt. No. 172; To create a point of order against legislation that would reduce Medicare and Medicaid benefits for Americans.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 276
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Baldwin Amdt. No. 276; To create a point of order against legislation that would take away health care from seniors, including those receiving care in nursing homes, through cuts to the Medicaid program.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 1156
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Wyden Amdt. No. 1156; To prevent millions of Americans from being kicked off their health coverage, suffering needlessly, getting sicker, and dying sooner.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 407
vote.title: Ossoff Amdt. No. 407; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting access to maternal and pediatric health care through Medicaid.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 776
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Schumer Amdt. No. 776; To prevent tax cuts for the wealthy if a single dollar of Medicaid funding is cut.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 1029
vote.title: Sullivan Amdt. No. 1029; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to protecting Medicare and Medicaid.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 316
vote.title: Schiff Amdt. No. 316; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to supporting Federal wildland firefighters and associated personnel.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 540
vote.title: Bennet Amdt. No. 540; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to reinstating the fired Federal employees at the Forest Service, National Park Service, United States Fish and Wildlife Service, and Bureau of Land Management.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 925
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Hickenlooper Amdt. No. 925; To create a point of order against legislation that would raise energy costs for Americans, including higher monthly electricity bills, building material expenses, and transportation costs.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 878
vote.title: Murray Amdt. No. 878; To strike the reconciliation instructions and create a reserve fund to implement a bipartisan, multi-year agreement to provide up to $171,000,000,000 in discretionary funding for defense and up to $171,000,000,000 in discretionary funding for other programs, accounts, and activities to address border, veterans, farmers, food and nutrition, disaster relief, and other needs.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 130
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Warner Amdt. No. 130; To create a point of order against any reconciliation bill that would not decrease the cost of housing for American families.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 473
vote.title: Merkley Amdt. No. 473; To establish a deficit-neutral reserve fund relating to the impacts of hedge fund ownership of single-family homes and rent prices.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 494
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Klobuchar Amdt. No. 494; To stop tax cuts for the ultra-rich while families struggle to put food on the table.

vote.question: On the Motion S.Amdt. 454
vote.title: Motion to Waive All Applicable Budgetary Discipline Re: Schumer Amdt. No. 454; To prevent unwarranted tax cuts for the ultra-rich.

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Douglas Burgum to be Secretary of the Interior

vote.question: On the Nomination
vote.title: Confirmation: Lee Zeldin, of N.Y., to be Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Lee Zeldin to be Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency

vote.question: On Cloture on the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Motion to Proceed to H.R. 23; A bill to impose sanctions with respect to the International Criminal Court engaged in any effort to investigate, arrest, detain, or prosecute any protected person of the United States and its allies.

vote.question: On the Nomination
vote.title: Confirmation: Sean Duffy, of WI, to be Secretary of Transportation

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Sean Duffy to be Secretary of Transportation

vote.title: Confirmation: Scott Bessent, of S.C., to be Secretary of the Treasury

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Scott Bessent to be Secretary of the Treasury

vote.title: Confirmation: Kristi Noem, of South Dakota, to be Secretary of Homeland Security

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Kristi Noem to be Secretary of Homeland Security

vote.question: On the Nomination
vote.title: Confirmation: Peter Hegseth, of Tennessee, to be Secretary of Defense

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Peter Hegseth to be Secretary of Defense

vote.title: Confirmation: John Ratcliffe, of Texas, to be Director of the Central Intelligence Agency

vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: John Ratcliffe to be Director of the Central Intelligence Agency

vote.question: On Cloture on the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Motion to Proceed to S.6; A bill to amend title 18, United States Code, to prohibit a health care practitioner from failing to exercise the proper degree of care in the case of a child who survives an abortion or attempted abortion.

vote.question: On the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Proceed to Executive Session to Consider the Nomination of Peter Hegseth to be Secretary of Defense

vote.question: On the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Proceed to Legislative Session

vote.question: On the Nomination
vote.title: Confirmation: Marco Rubio, of Florida, to be Secretary of State

vote.question: On Passage of the Bill
vote.title: S. 5, As Amended; A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to take into custody aliens who have been charged in the United States with theft, and for other purposes.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 8
vote.title: Ernst Amdt No. 8, As Amended; To include crimes resulting in death or serious bodily injury to the list of offenses that, if committed by an inadmissible alien, require mandatory detention.

vote.question: On the Cloture Motion
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: S. 5; A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to take into custody aliens who have been charged in the United States with theft, and for other purposes.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 23
vote.title: Coons Amdt. No. 23; To strike the section that authorizes State attorneys general to sue Federal immigration authorities for alleged violations relating to the detention of aliens.

vote.question: On the Amendment S.Amdt. 14
vote.title: Cornyn Amdt. No. 14; To expand the list of criminal offenses that subject inadmissible aliens to mandatory detention.

vote.question: On the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Proceed to S. 5; A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to take into custody aliens who have been charged in the United States with theft, and for other purposes.

vote.question: On Cloture on the Motion to Proceed
vote.title: Motion to Invoke Cloture: Motion to Proceed to S. 5; A bill to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to take into custody aliens who have been charged in the United States with theft, and for other purposes.
